contentBlocks.original:
दोहा
जयकाली कलिमलहरण, महिमा अगम अपार ।
महिष मर्दिनी कालिका , देहु अभय अपार ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dōhā
jayakālī kalimalaharaṇa, mahimā agama apāra ।
mahiṣa mardinī kālikā , dēhu abhaya apāra ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Opening doha: Victory to Kāḷī, remover of the filth of the Kali age — your glory is boundless and unfathomable! O Kālikā, crusher of Mahiṣa, grant unlimited fearlessness.

contentBlocks.original:
अरि मद मान मिटावन हारी ।
मुण्डमाल गल सोहत प्यारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ari mada māna miṭāvana hārī ।
muṇḍamāla gala sōhata pyārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 1: Destroyer of the pride and arrogance of enemies. The garland of skulls gleams beautifully around your neck.

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टभुजी सुखदायक माता ।
दुष्टदलन जग में विख्याता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭabhujī sukhadāyaka mātā ।
duṣṭadalana jaga mēṃ vikhyātā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 2: O eight-armed Mother, bestower of happiness. Famous throughout the world as the crusher of the wicked.

contentBlocks.original:
भाल विशाल मुकुट छविछाजै ।
कर में शीश शत्रु का साजै ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāla viśāla mukuṭa ChaviChājai ।
kara mēṃ śīśa śatru kā sājai ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 3: A great crown adorns your broad forehead beautifully. In your hand you hold an enemy's severed head.

contentBlocks.original:
दूजे हाथ लिए मधु प्याला ।
हाथ तीसरे सोहत भाला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dūjē hātha liē madhu pyālā ।
hātha tīsarē sōhata bhālā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 4: In the second hand you hold a cup of wine. In the third hand a spear gleams resplendently.

contentBlocks.original:
चौथे खप्पर खड्ग कर पाञ्चे ।
छठे त्रिशूलशत्रु बल जाञ्चे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chauthē khappara khaḍga kara pāñchē ।
Chaṭhē triśūlaśatru bala jāñchē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 5: In the fourth hand a skull-bowl; in the fifth a sword. In the sixth — the trident — that tests the strength of enemies.

contentBlocks.original:
सप्तम करदमकत असि प्यारी ।
शोभा अद्भुत मात तुम्हारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saptama karadamakata asi pyārī ।
śōbhā adbhuta māta tumhārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 6: In the seventh hand a beloved gleaming blade. Your beauty, O Mother, is utterly wondrous.

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टम कर भक्तन वर दाता ।
जग मनहरण रूप ये माता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭama kara bhaktana vara dātā ।
jaga manaharaṇa rūpa yē mātā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 7: The eighth hand bestows boons upon devotees. This is your heart-stealing form, O Mother.

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तन में अनुरक्त भवानी ।
निशदिन रटेंॠषी-मुनि ज्ञानी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktana mēṃ anurakta bhavānī ।
niśadina raṭēṃṝṣī-muni jñānī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 8: O Bhavānī, deeply devoted to your devotees. Wise sages and seers repeat your name day and night.

contentBlocks.original:
महशक्ति अति प्रबल पुनीता ।
तू ही काली तू ही सीता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahaśakti ati prabala punītā ।
tū hī kālī tū hī sītā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 9: Great power — supremely strong and supremely pure. You are Kāḷī herself; you are also Sītā.

contentBlocks.original:
पतित तारिणी हे जग पालक ।
कल्याणी पापीकुल घालक ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
patita tāriṇī hē jaga pālaka ।
kalyāṇī pāpīkula ghālaka ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 10: O lifter of the fallen, O sustainer of the world. O Kaḷyāṇī, destroyer of the sinful.

contentBlocks.original:
शेष सुरेश न पावत पारा ।
गौरी रूप धर्यो इक बारा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śēṣa surēśa na pāvata pārā ।
gaurī rūpa dharyō ika bārā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 11: Even Śēṣa and Indra cannot fathom your depths. Once you assumed the gentle form of Gaurī.

contentBlocks.original:
तुम समान दाता नहिं दूजा ।
विधिवत करें भक्तजन पूजा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tuma samāna dātā nahiṃ dūjā ।
vidhivata karēṃ bhaktajana pūjā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 12: There is no giver equal to you in all the world. Devotees worship you according to prescribed rites.

contentBlocks.original:
रूप भयङ्कर जब तुम धारा ।
दुष्टदलन कीन्हेहु संहारा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rūpa bhayaṅkara jaba tuma dhārā ।
duṣṭadalana kīnhēhu saṃhārā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 13: When you assumed the terrifying form, you carried out the destruction of all the wicked.

contentBlocks.original:
नाम अनेकन मात तुम्हारे ।
भक्तजनों के सङ्कट टारे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāma anēkana māta tumhārē ।
bhaktajanōṃ kē saṅkaṭa ṭārē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 14: Your names are countless, O Mother. They remove the troubles of all devotees.

contentBlocks.original:
कलि के कष्ट कलेशन हरनी ।
भव भय मोचन मङ्गल करनी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kali kē kaṣṭa kalēśana haranī ।
bhava bhaya mōchana maṅgala karanī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 15: Remover of the suffering and afflictions of the Kali age. Liberator from the fears of worldly existence, bringer of auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original:
महिमा अगम वेद यश गावैम् ।
नारद शारद पार न पावैम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahimā agama vēda yaśa gāvaim ।
nārada śārada pāra na pāvaim ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 16: Your unfathomable glory is sung by the Vedas themselves. Even Nārada and Sarasvatī cannot reach its end.

contentBlocks.original:
भू पर भार बढ्यौ जब भारी ।
तब तब तुम प्रकटीं महतारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhū para bhāra baḍhyau jaba bhārī ।
taba taba tuma prakaṭīṃ mahatārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 17: Whenever the burden on the earth grew too heavy, again and again you appeared, O great Mother.

contentBlocks.original:
आदि अनादि अभय वरदाता ।
विश्वविदित भव सङ्कट त्राता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādi anādi abhaya varadātā ।
viśvavidita bhava saṅkaṭa trātā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 18: Beginningless and endless — giver of fearlessness and boons. Known throughout the universe as the savior from all existential crises.

contentBlocks.original:
कुसमय नाम तुम्हारौ लीन्हा ।
उसको सदा अभय वर दीन्हा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kusamaya nāma tumhārau līnhā ।
usakō sadā abhaya vara dīnhā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 19: Whoever has taken your name in a time of crisis — you have always granted them the boon of fearlessness.

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यान धरें श्रुति शेष सुरेशा ।
काल रूप लखि तुमरो भेषा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāna dharēṃ śruti śēṣa surēśā ।
kāla rūpa lakhi tumarō bhēṣā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 20: The Vedas, Śēṣa, and Indra meditate on you. They perceive your form as the very guise of cosmic Time.

contentBlocks.original:
कलुआ भैंरों सङ्ग तुम्हारे ।
अरि हित रूप भयानक धारे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaluā bhaiṃrōṃ saṅga tumhārē ।
ari hita rūpa bhayānaka dhārē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 21: Kālabhairava accompanies you. He assumes a terrifying form to destroy your enemies.

contentBlocks.original:
सेवक लाङ्गुर रहत अगारी ।
चौसठ जोगन आज्ञाकारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sēvaka lāṅgura rahata agārī ।
chausaṭha jōgana ājñākārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 22: The Laṅgūra attendants walk before you. The sixty-four Yoginīs execute your every command.

contentBlocks.original:
त्रेता में रघुवर हित आई ।
दशकन्धर की सैन नसाई ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trētā mēṃ raghuvara hita āī ।
daśakandhara kī saina nasāī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 23: In the Tretā age you came for the sake of Rāma. You routed the entire army of the ten-headed Rāvaṇa.

contentBlocks.original:
खेला रण का खेल निराला ।
भरा मांस-मज्जा से प्याला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
khēlā raṇa kā khēla nirālā ।
bharā māṃsa-majjā sē pyālā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 24: You played the unique game of battle — filling your cup with flesh and marrow.

contentBlocks.original:
रौद्र रूप लखि दानव भागे ।
कियौ गवन भवन निज त्यागे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raudra rūpa lakhi dānava bhāgē ।
kiyau gavana bhavana nija tyāgē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 25: Seeing your fierce form, the demons fled — they abandoned their own homes and ran away.

contentBlocks.original:
तब ऐसौ तामस चढ़ आयो ।
स्वजन विजन को भेद भुलायो ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taba aisau tāmasa chaḍha़ āyō ।
svajana vijana kō bhēda bhulāyō ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 26: Then such dark tamas (delusion) rose within you — you forgot the distinction between kinsman and stranger.

contentBlocks.original:
ये बालक लखि शङ्कर आए ।
राह रोक चरनन में धाए ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē bālaka lakhi śaṅkara āē ।
rāha rōka charanana mēṃ dhāē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 27: Seeing this, Śaṅkara (Śiva) came forward. He blocked your path and fell at your feet.

contentBlocks.original:
तब मुख जीभ निकर जो आई ।
यही रूप प्रचलित है माई ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taba mukha jībha nikara jō āī ।
yahī rūpa prachalita hai māī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 28: Then, with Śiva lying beneath you, your tongue came out. O Mother, this is the celebrated image known throughout the world.

contentBlocks.original:
बाढ्यो महिषासुर मद भारी ।
पीड़इत किए सकल नर-नारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bāḍhyō mahiṣāsura mada bhārī ।
pīḍa़ita kiē sakala nara-nārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 29: Mahiṣāsura's arrogance grew exceedingly great. He tormented all men and women.

contentBlocks.original:
करूण पुकार सुनी भक्तन की ।
पीर मिटावन हित जन-जन की ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karūṇa pukāra sunī bhaktana kī ।
pīra miṭāvana hita jana-jana kī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 30: You heard the pitiful cry of your devotees — to remove the pain of every person —

contentBlocks.original:
तब प्रगटी निज सैन समेता ।
नाम पड़आ मां महिष विजेता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taba pragaṭī nija saina samētā ।
nāma paḍa़ā māṃ mahiṣa vijētā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 31: — you appeared with your full army. Thus you earned the name Mahiṣavijētā (conqueror of Mahiṣa).

contentBlocks.original:
शुम्भ निशुम्भ हने छन माहीम् ।
तुम सम जग दूसर कौ नाहीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śumbha niśumbha hanē Chana māhīm ।
tuma sama jaga dūsara kau nāhīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 32: You slew Śumbha and Niśumbha in an instant. In all the world there is none other like you.

contentBlocks.original:
मान मथनहारी खल दल के ।
सदा सहायक भक्त विकल के ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māna mathanahārī khala dala kē ।
sadā sahāyaka bhakta vikala kē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 33: Crusher of the pride of the wicked. Ever the helper of devotees who are distressed.

contentBlocks.original:
दीन विहीन करैं नित सेवा ।
पावैं मनवाञ्छित फल मेवा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīna vihīna karaiṃ nita sēvā ।
pāvaiṃ manavāñChita phala mēvā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 34: Those who serve you daily, though poor and destitute — they receive the sweet fruit of their heart's desire.

contentBlocks.original:
सङ्कट में जो सुमिरन करहीम् ।
उनके कष्ट मातु तुम हरहीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṅkaṭa mēṃ jō sumirana karahīm ।
unakē kaṣṭa mātu tuma harahīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 35: Those who remember you in their hour of crisis — O Mother, you remove their afflictions.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रेम सहित जो कीरतिगावैम् ।
भव बन्धन सों मुक्ती पावैम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prēma sahita jō kīratigāvaim ।
bhava bandhana sōṃ muktī pāvaim ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 36: Those who lovingly sing your glory — they gain liberation from the bondage of worldly existence.

contentBlocks.original:
काली चालीसा जो पढ़हीम् ।
स्वर्गलोक बिनु बन्धन चढ़हीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kālī chālīsā jō paḍha़hīm ।
svargalōka binu bandhana chaḍha़hīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 37: Whoever reads this Kālī Cālīsā — they ascend to the heavenly realm, free of all bondage.

contentBlocks.original:
दया दृष्टि हेरौ जगदम्बा ।
केहि कारणमां कियौ विलम्बा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dayā dṛṣṭi hērau jagadambā ।
kēhi kāraṇamāṃ kiyau vilambā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 38: Turn your merciful gaze upon me, O Jagadambā. For what reason have you delayed, O Mother?

contentBlocks.original:
करहु मातु भक्तन रखवाली ।
जयति जयति काली कङ्काली ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karahu mātu bhaktana rakhavālī ।
jayati jayati kālī kaṅkālī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 39: O Mother, keep watch over your devotees. Victory, victory to Kāḷī, to Kaṅkālī!

contentBlocks.original:
सेवक दीन अनाथ अनारी।
भक्तिभाव युति शरण तुम्हारी ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sēvaka dīna anātha anārī।
bhaktibhāva yuti śaraṇa tumhārī ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 40: Your servant is poor, orphaned, and without resource. With sincere devotion I take refuge at your feet.

contentBlocks.original:
दोहा
प्रेम सहित जो करे, काली चालीसा पाठ ।
तिनकी पूरन कामना, होय सकल जग ठाठ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dōhā
prēma sahita jō karē, kālī chālīsā pāṭha ।
tinakī pūrana kāmanā, hōya sakala jaga ṭhāṭha ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Closing doha — Phalaśruti: Whoever recites the Kālī Cālīsā with love — their every desire is fulfilled, and all the world prospers.
